topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) None Course Type Lab Course Objectives

1. To apply and analyze Embedded C/Assembly Language Programming techniques for modern micro controllers. 2. To develop the ability to interface microcontrollers through Embedded C or Assembly Language Programming. 3. To apply concepts related to IoT system using Arduino/Raspberry Pi/open platform. Expt. No. Experiments Part A- Microcontroller Lab ( Minimum 5 experiments) Conduct ANY FIVE of the following experiments on an ARM Cortex-M3/M4 evaluation board using ARM assembly/Embedded 'C' & Keil μ Vision tool/compiler or using 8051 Simulators or other similar tools. 1 Display “Hello World” message using Internal UART 2 Interface and Control a DC Motor 3 Interface a Stepper motor and rotate it in clockwise and anti-clockwise direction 4 Interface a DAC and generate Triangular and Square waveforms. 5 Interface a 4x4 keyboard and display the key code on an LCD. 6 Using the Internal PWM module of ARM controller generate PWM and vary its duty cycle. 7 Demonstrate the use of an external interrupt to toggle an LED On/Off. 8 Display the Hex digits 0 to F on a 7-segment LED interface
with an appropriate delay in between. 9 Interface a simple Switch and display its status through Relay
Buzzer and LED Course Assessment Method (CIE

The external examiner shall endorse the record Part B- IoT Lab( Minimum 5 experiments) 10 Familiarization with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and perform necessary software installation 11 To interface LED/Buzzer with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and write a program to turn ON LED for 1 sec after every 2 second. 12 To interface Push button/Digital sensor (IR/LDR) with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and write a program to turn ON LED when push button is pressed or at sensor detection. 13 To interface DHT11 sensor with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and write a program to print temperature and humidity readings. 14 To interface Bluetooth with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and write a program to send sensor data to smartphone using Bluetooth.. 15 To interface Bluetooth with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and write a program to turn LED ON/OFF when 1‘/‘0‘ is received from smartphone using Bluetooth 16 Write a program on Arduino/Raspberry Pi to upload temperature and humidity data to Thing speak cloud 17 Write a program on Arduino/Raspberry Pi to retrieve temperature and humidity data from thing speak cloud. Course Outcomes (COs) At the end of the course students should be able to

Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 Apply basic programming concepts to develop and debug programs utilizing ARM Cortex-M3/M4 and 8051 microcontrollers. K3 CO2 Utilize internal modules like UART

and timers in ARM microcontrollers. K3 CO3 Design and build a system to Interface and control motors using microcontrollers. K6 CO4 Create and analyze waveforms through the use of DAC interfaces. K4 CO5 Develop programs IoT applications using Arduino/Raspberry P. K3 CO6 Design of solutions to control various sensors and modules with Arduino/Raspberry Pi for IoT applications. Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved. ● Preliminary Work and Planning
Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment. ● Algorithm Development

Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment. ● Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks) ● Setup and Execution
Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks) ● Accuracy of Results
Precision and correctness of the obtained results. ● Analysis and Interpretation
Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks) ● Ability to explain the experiment
